title: Lauds
description: Morning praise. The Church greets the rising sun as a figure of the Risen Christ.

sections.title: Opening
sections.rubric: Lauds begins immediately after Matins, or if said separately:
sections.latinText:
V. Deus, in adiutórium meum inténde.
R. Dómine, ad adiuvándum me festína.
Glória Patri, et Fílio, et Spirítui Sancto. Sicut erat in princípio, et nunc, et semper, et in sǽcula sæculórum. Amen. Allelúia.
sections.englishText:
V. O God, come to my assistance.
R. O Lord, make haste to help me.
Glory be to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Ghost. As it was in the beginning, is now, and ever shall be, world without end. Amen. Alleluia.

sections.title: Psalms & Canticle
sections.rubric: Five psalms are recited with their proper antiphons. The psalms change by the day of the week and by feast. On Sunday: Psalms 92, 99, the Canticle of the Three Children (Dan 3:57-88), Psalms 148, 149, 150.
sections.latinText: [Proper — five psalms with antiphons, varying by day and feast]
sections.englishText: [Proper — five psalms with antiphons, varying by day and feast]

sections.title: Short Chapter
sections.rubric: The chapter varies by season and feast.
sections.latinText:
[Proper — changes by season]

Outside special seasons (Romans 13:12-13):
Nox præcéssit, dies autem appropinquávit. Abiiciámus ergo ópera tenebrárum, et induámur arma lucis. Sicut in die honéste ambulémus.
R. Deo grátias.
sections.englishText:
[Proper — changes by season]

Outside special seasons (Romans 13:12-13):
The night is passed, and the day is at hand. Let us therefore cast off the works of darkness, and put on the armor of light. Let us walk honestly, as in the day.
R. Thanks be to God.

sections.title: Hymn
sections.rubric: The hymn varies by day and season. On Sunday outside special seasons:
sections.latinText:
Ætérne rerum Cónditor,
Noctem diémque qui regis,
Et témporum das témpora,
Ut álleves fastídium.

Præco diéi iam sonat,
Noctis profúndæ pérvigil,
Noctúrna lux viántibus,
A nocte noctem ségregans.

Hoc excitátus Lúcifer
Solvit polum calígine:
Hoc omnis errónum chorus
Vias nocéndi déserit.

Deo Patri sit glória,
Eiúsque soli Fílio,
Cum Spíritu Paráclito,
Nunc et per omne sǽculum. Amen.
sections.englishText:
Framer of the earth and sky,
Ruler of the day and night,
With a glad variety
Tempering all, and making light;

Gleams upon our dark path flinging,
Watchman of the hours of night,
With the cock's familiar singing
Severing the darkness from the light.

Roused at his warning call,
The Morning Star shines bright and clear;
The host of evil spirits all
Leave off their wandering here and there.

To God the Father, glory great,
And glory to the Son divine,
With the Holy Paraclete,
Now and through all ages shine. Amen.

sections.title: Canticle of Zachary: Benedictus
sections.rubric: Luke 1:68-79. All make the Sign of the Cross at the beginning. This canticle is the climax of Lauds.
sections.latinText:
Benedíctus Dóminus, Deus Israël, quia visitávit et fecit redemptiónem plebis suæ:
Et eréxit cornu salútis nobis in domo David, púeri sui.
Sicut locútus est per os sanctórum, qui a sǽculo sunt, prophetárum eius:
Salútem ex inimícis nostris, et de manu ómnium, qui odérunt nos.
Ad faciéndam misericórdiam cum pátribus nostris: et memorári testaménti sui sancti.
Iusiurándum, quod iurávit ad Abraham patrem nostrum, datúrum se nobis:
Ut sine timóre, de manu inimicórum nostrórum liberáti, serviámus illi.
In sanctitáte et iustítia coram ipso, ómnibus diébus nostris.
Et tu, puer, Prophéta Altíssimi vocáberis: præíbis enim ante fáciem Dómini paráre vias eius:
Ad dandam sciéntiam salútis plebi eius: in remissiónem peccatórum eórum:
Per víscera misericórdiæ Dei nostri: in quibus visitávit nos, óriens ex alto:
Illumináre his, qui in ténebris et in umbra mortis sedent: ad dirigéndos pedes nostros in viam pacis.
Glória Patri, et Fílio, et Spirítui Sancto. Sicut erat in princípio, et nunc, et semper, et in sǽcula sæculórum. Amen.
sections.englishText:
Blessed be the Lord God of Israel: because He hath visited and wrought the redemption of His people:
And hath raised up a horn of salvation to us, in the house of David His servant.
As He spoke by the mouth of His holy prophets, who are from the beginning:
Salvation from our enemies, and from the hand of all that hate us.
To perform mercy to our fathers, and to remember His holy testament.
The oath, which He swore to Abraham our father, that He would grant to us:
That being delivered from the hand of our enemies, we may serve Him without fear.
In holiness and justice before Him, all our days.
And thou, child, shalt be called the prophet of the Highest: for thou shalt go before the face of the Lord to prepare His ways:
To give knowledge of salvation to His people, unto the remission of their sins:
Through the bowels of the mercy of our God, in which the Orient from on high hath visited us:
To enlighten them that sit in darkness, and in the shadow of death: to direct our feet into the way of peace.
Glory be to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Ghost. As it was in the beginning, is now, and ever shall be, world without end. Amen.

sections.title: Closing Prayers
sections.latinText:
V. Dóminus vobíscum. R. Et cum spíritu tuo.
Orémus.
[Proper collect of the day]
R. Amen.
V. Dóminus vobíscum. R. Et cum spíritu tuo.
V. Benedicámus Dómino. R. Deo grátias.
V. Fidélium ánimæ, per misericórdiam Dei, requiéscant in pace. R. Amen.
sections.englishText:
V. The Lord be with you. R. And with thy spirit.
Let us pray.
[Proper collect of the day]
R. Amen.
V. The Lord be with you. R. And with thy spirit.
V. Let us bless the Lord. R. Thanks be to God.
V. May the souls of the faithful departed, through the mercy of God, rest in peace. R. Amen.
